SynPrimitiveOccurrenceScanner.docs: Syn-based implementation of domain::tddd::primitive_occurrence_scanner::PrimitiveOccurrenceScanner (ADR 2026-07-01-0004 D2/D3; spec AC-02/AC-03/AC-04, IN-02/IN-03/IN-05). Reuses the existing type_ref_parser syn-parsing machinery (CN-01) to parse a TypeRef's string into a syn type, then performs a full recursive syn::visit::Visit walk with no per-position special-casing to find every ident matching a name in the requested primitive set at every position (named struct field, enum variant field, method/function param, return, bound, type_alias target, and nested inside transparent containers such as Option / Vec / Box / Arc / BTreeMap and function-type signatures Fn / FnMut / FnOnce). ident matching is exact (no substring matches). Each occurrence is labelled with the PrimitiveOccurrencePosition it was found at and returned in a position-keyed PrimitiveOccurrenceReport: occurrences found as the second (Err) type argument of a two-argument Result path segment are labelled ResultErr regardless of nesting depth (e.g. Vec<Result<T, String>>); occurrences found while descending into a nested function-type signature's own parameter / return slots, or a nested generic-bound clause, are labelled Param / Return / Bound respectively regardless of the caller-supplied `position` argument; all other (top-level, not otherwise reclassified) occurrences are labelled with whatever `position` the caller passed to scan (NamedField / VariantField / Param / Return / Bound / TypeAliasTarget) -- so a nested Param occurrence (e.g. inside a Box<dyn Fn(String) -> _> field) is distinguishable in the resulting report from the field's own outer NamedField label instead of collapsing together. If `position` is PrimitiveOccurrencePosition::ResultErr, returns PrimitiveOccurrenceScanError::InvalidSitePosition instead of scanning, since ResultErr is exclusively scan-intrinsic. Stateless: holds no fields, so no constructor beyond Default is needed.
